advocacy_campaigns.title: Access to Affordable CF Medications
advocacy_campaigns.short_description: Urge Congress to protect and expand access to life-saving cystic fibrosis medications.
advocacy_campaigns.full_description:
Breakthrough cystic fibrosis (CF) treatments have transformed life expectancy, but too many people still cannot afford the medications their doctors prescribe. Rising out-of-pocket costs, insurance barriers, and gaps in public programs put families in impossible situations—forcing them to choose between essential CF therapies and basic necessities.

This campaign asks Congress and federal agencies to protect patient access by capping out-of-pocket costs, strengthening insurance protections, and ensuring that new CF therapies are accessible to everyone who needs them, regardless of income or insurance status.

Use the form below to send a personalized message to your U.S. Senators and urge them to support policies that keep CF medications affordable.
advocacy_campaigns.message_template:
Dear Senator,

[[PERSONALIZE_FIRST_PARAGRAPH_HERE]] People living with cystic fibrosis rely on a complex regimen of medications and therapies every single day to stay as healthy and independent as possible. When these medications are unaffordable, families are forced into dangerous trade-offs that can lead to avoidable hospitalizations and long-term health decline.

I urge you to support policies that protect and expand access to affordable CF medications. This includes capping out-of-pocket costs, preventing insurers from placing essential CF drugs on the highest cost-sharing tiers, and safeguarding coverage for people with pre-existing conditions.

Please stand with the CF community by prioritizing access and affordability in any health care legislation you consider. Your leadership can help ensure that cost is never a barrier to life-saving treatment for people with cystic fibrosis.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[City, State]

advocacy_campaigns.title: Protect Insurance Coverage for People with CF
advocacy_campaigns.short_description: Tell lawmakers to preserve strong insurance protections for people with cystic fibrosis.
advocacy_campaigns.full_description:
People with cystic fibrosis depend on comprehensive health coverage for medications, clinic visits, hospitalizations, and specialized therapies. Proposals that weaken protections for people with pre-existing conditions or allow skimpy plans can put individuals with CF at serious risk.

This campaign urges policymakers to preserve and strengthen insurance protections at both the federal and state levels, including bans on annual and lifetime limits and protections against coverage denials.
advocacy_campaigns.message_template:
Dear [Official],

[[PERSONALIZE_FIRST_PARAGRAPH_HERE]] As you consider changes to our health care system, I ask you to ensure that people living with cystic fibrosis continue to have access to comprehensive, affordable insurance coverage.

Strong protections for people with pre-existing conditions, as well as bans on annual and lifetime limits, are essential for the CF community. Without these safeguards, many individuals with CF could lose access to the treatments that keep them stable and out of the hospital.

Please oppose any legislation that would weaken these protections, and support policies that make coverage more affordable and comprehensive for people living with serious chronic illnesses like cystic fibrosis.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[City, State]

advocacy_campaigns.title: Increase Federal Funding for CF Research
advocacy_campaigns.short_description: Ask Congress to boost federal investment in CF research at NIH and other agencies.
advocacy_campaigns.full_description:
Life expectancy for people with cystic fibrosis has improved dramatically thanks to decades of research—but we are still far from a cure. Continued progress depends on sustained and increased funding for CF research at the National Institutes of Health and other federal agencies.

This campaign calls on Congress to prioritize CF research funding so that future generations can live longer, healthier lives.
advocacy_campaigns.message_template:
Dear Member of Congress,

[[PERSONALIZE_FIRST_PARAGRAPH_HERE]] Federal investment in medical research has already transformed cystic fibrosis from a fatal childhood illness into a condition where many people are reaching adulthood and beyond.

Yet too many people with CF still face severe symptoms, complications, and shortened lives. Continued progress depends on robust funding for CF research at the National Institutes of Health and other agencies that support clinical trials, basic science, and real-world outcomes research.

I urge you to support increased funding for CF research in the upcoming federal budget and to champion policies that accelerate the development of new and better treatments.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[City, State]

advocacy_campaigns.title: Support Cystic Fibrosis Awareness Month
advocacy_campaigns.short_description: Encourage your local officials to recognize May as Cystic Fibrosis Awareness Month.
advocacy_campaigns.full_description:
Public awareness is critical to driving support for research, better policies, and inclusive communities for people with cystic fibrosis. By issuing proclamations or resolutions, local and state officials can shine a spotlight on CF and the challenges families face.

Use this tool to ask your mayor, governor, or other local officials to recognize May as Cystic Fibrosis Awareness Month.
advocacy_campaigns.message_template:
Dear [Official],

[[PERSONALIZE_FIRST_PARAGRAPH_HERE]] I am writing to request that you recognize May as Cystic Fibrosis Awareness Month in our community.

A formal proclamation or resolution would help raise awareness about cystic fibrosis, honor those living with the disease, and highlight the ongoing need for research, support services, and inclusive policies.

Thank you for considering this request and for your leadership on issues that affect families impacted by chronic illness.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[City, State]

articles.title: Nutrition Foundations for Adults Living with Cystic Fibrosis
articles.summary: Key nutrition strategies to support lung health, weight, and energy levels in adults with CF.
articles.body:
Good nutrition is a cornerstone of care for adults with cystic fibrosis. Because CF affects how your body absorbs fat and protein, you may need more calories than people without CF just to maintain your weight.

Work with your CF care team and a registered dietitian to set personal calorie and protein goals. Many adults with CF benefit from three main meals plus two to three snacks per day. Including a source of fat and protein at each meal and snack can help stabilize energy levels.

Pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy (PERT) is critical if you have pancreatic insufficiency. Take your enzymes with all meals and snacks that contain fat or protein, and talk with your team if you notice symptoms like bloating, gas, or oily stools—these may be signs that your dose needs adjustment.

Hydration and salt intake also matter. Because people with CF lose more salt in their sweat, you may need extra salty foods or salt tablets, especially in hot weather or during exercise. Ask your team for guidance based on your health, medications, and activity level.

articles.title: Meal Planning Tips for Adults with CF on Busy Schedules
articles.summary: Practical ways to fit high-calorie, high-protein meals into real life when you have CF.
articles.body:
Balancing work, school, family, and health care appointments can make it hard to prioritize nutrition, especially when you live with cystic fibrosis. A bit of planning can make high-calorie, high-protein eating more manageable.

Start by identifying your toughest meal of the day—often breakfast or lunch—and focus on that first. Prep grab-and-go options like breakfast burritos, trail mix with nuts and dried fruit, or full-fat yogurt with granola and seeds. Keep enzyme doses in your bag, at work, and in the car so you’re less likely to miss them.

Batch cooking can help on days when you have more energy. Cook extra portions of high-calorie meals such as casseroles, soups, or pasta dishes and freeze individual servings. Add healthy fats like olive oil, butter, cheese, or nut butters to boost calories without increasing portion size too much.

If you struggle with appetite, schedule regular “eating reminders” on your phone and aim for smaller, more frequent meals. Liquid nutrition supplements or shakes can be a convenient option when you’re short on time or feeling tired.

articles.title: How to Read Nutrition Labels When You Have CF
articles.summary: Learn which parts of the nutrition label matter most for people with cystic fibrosis.
articles.body:
Nutrition labels can feel overwhelming, but a few key sections are especially important when you have cystic fibrosis. Start with calories, fat, and protein. Many adults with CF need higher-calorie foods with substantial fat and protein to maintain weight and muscle.

Check the serving size so you know how much you’re actually eating. If you usually eat more than one serving, multiply the calories, fat, protein, and sodium accordingly. For example, if a frozen meal lists 400 calories per serving and you typically eat two servings, you’re getting 800 calories.

Look at saturated fat and added sugars in context. Some saturated fat is acceptable—especially if you’re underweight—but talk with your team about heart health and cholesterol, particularly if you’re taking CFTR modulators. Added sugars can be useful for quick calories, but large amounts may affect blood sugar, especially if you have CF-related diabetes.

articles.title: Eating Well at School: Nutrition Tips for Teens with CF
articles.summary: Strategies for getting enough calories and enzymes during the school day.
articles.body:
School days can be long and busy, and it’s easy for teens with cystic fibrosis to miss meals, snacks, or enzyme doses. Planning ahead can help you get the calories and nutrients you need without drawing unwanted attention.

Pack high-calorie, high-protein snacks you enjoy, such as cheese and crackers, trail mix, or peanut butter sandwiches. Talk with a trusted teacher or school nurse about where you can store enzyme bottles and how to step out to take them if needed.

If your appetite is low at lunchtime, consider eating a smaller meal with a higher-calorie snack later in the afternoon. Work with your CF dietitian to build a flexible plan that fits your schedule and comfort level.

articles.title: Getting Started with Exercise as an Adult with CF
articles.summary: How to begin or restart an exercise routine safely when you live with CF.
articles.body:
Exercise can support lung function, mood, and overall health for adults with cystic fibrosis, but it’s important to tailor your routine to your abilities and health status. Before starting, talk with your CF care team about what types of activity are safe for you and whether you need any tests, such as a baseline exercise test.

Begin slowly with activities you enjoy, like walking, dancing at home, or light cycling. Aim for short sessions—10 to 15 minutes—several times per week and gradually increase duration and intensity. Focus on breathing techniques during exercise, and take breaks as needed.

Hydration and salt intake are crucial. Because people with CF lose extra salt when they sweat, you may need to drink electrolyte-rich fluids or add salty snacks before and after workouts. Monitor for symptoms like dizziness, headaches, or cramps and report them to your care team.

articles.title: Strength Training to Support Lung Health in CF
articles.summary: Why building muscle can help adults with CF feel stronger and breathe easier.
articles.body:
Strength training isn’t just for athletes. For adults with cystic fibrosis, maintaining muscle mass can support posture, breathing, and everyday activities like climbing stairs or carrying groceries.

Start with bodyweight exercises such as sit-to-stands, wall push-ups, and step-ups. Focus on good form and breathing control rather than heavy weights. As you build confidence, you can add resistance bands or light dumbbells.

Aim for two to three strength sessions per week on non-consecutive days. If you have joint pain, osteoporosis, or chronic pain, your CF team or a physical therapist can help you select safe exercises and adjust intensity.

articles.title: Creating a 504 Plan for Students with Cystic Fibrosis
articles.summary: How families can work with schools to set up accommodations for students living with CF.
articles.body:
A 504 plan outlines accommodations that help a student with a health condition, like cystic fibrosis, fully participate in school. Common supports include extra restroom breaks, access to water and snacks, a place to store medications, and flexibility for absences due to illness or clinic visits.

To start the process, contact your school’s counselor, nurse, or 504 coordinator and provide a letter from your CF care team explaining your child’s needs. During the 504 meeting, discuss specific accommodations and how they will be communicated to teachers.

Review the plan at least once per year or whenever your child’s health needs change. Encourage your child to share what helps them feel safe and included at school.

articles.title: Transitioning to College with Cystic Fibrosis
articles.summary: Planning ahead for housing, academics, and health care when heading to college.
articles.body:
Going to college is a big transition for any young person, and living with cystic fibrosis adds extra layers of planning. Start early by meeting with your CF team to discuss transferring care to an adult CF center, medication refills, and emergency plans.

On campus, connect with disability services to explore accommodations like priority housing, reduced course loads, or flexible attendance policies. Think through practical details such as where you’ll store medications and supplies, how you’ll handle cleaning treatments in shared spaces, and how to talk with roommates about infection prevention.

It can be helpful to practice managing appointments, insurance, and prescription refills while you’re still at home so you feel more confident when you arrive at school.

articles.title: Why Mental Health Screening Matters in CF Care
articles.summary: An overview of depression and anxiety screening for people with CF and their caregivers.
articles.body:
Living with cystic fibrosis can be emotionally demanding. That’s why CF care guidelines recommend routine screening for depression and anxiety in people with CF and their caregivers.

Screening usually involves short questionnaires that ask about mood, sleep, energy, and worries. These tools help your team identify when you might benefit from extra support, such as counseling, peer support, or medication.

If you receive a positive screen, it doesn’t mean something is “wrong” with you—it simply opens the door to more conversation. You have options, and your CF social worker or psychologist can help you decide what feels right.

articles.title: Caring for Yourself While Caring for a Child with CF
articles.summary: Practical ideas for parents and caregivers to support their own well-being.
articles.body:
Parents and caregivers of children with cystic fibrosis often put their own needs last. Over time, constant caregiving, coordinating appointments, and worrying about the future can lead to burnout.

Building in small, realistic moments of self-care can make a difference. This might include a weekly walk with a friend, a regular therapy appointment, or simply protecting 15 minutes before bed for reading or quiet time.

Ask your CF team about caregiver support groups, respite resources, or counseling options. Taking care of yourself is not selfish—it’s an essential part of caring for your child.

articles.title: Understanding Common CF Medications
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to airway clearance medications, enzymes, and modulators.
articles.body:
Most people with cystic fibrosis take several medications every day to support lung function, digestion, and overall health. These often include airway clearance medicines (like bronchodilators and mucolytics), antibiotics, pancreatic enzymes, vitamins, and CFTR modulators.

It can help to group your medications by purpose and time of day. Use a medication schedule or app to keep track of doses. Bring all of your medications to clinic visits so your team can review them and check for side effects or interactions.

If cost is a barrier, talk with your social worker or pharmacist about financial assistance programs and insurance appeals.

articles.title: CFTR Modulators: What to Expect When Starting Treatment
articles.summary: Information about starting CFTR modulators, monitoring, and side effects.
articles.body:
CFTR modulators target the underlying protein problem in many forms of cystic fibrosis. If you’re eligible for a modulator, your care team will discuss which medicine is appropriate, how to take it, and what tests you need before and after starting.

Common monitoring includes liver function tests and eye exams in certain age groups. Some people notice improvements in breathing, weight, and energy within days or weeks, while others experience a more gradual change.

Side effects can include headaches, stomach upset, or mood changes. Report new or concerning symptoms promptly so your team can adjust your plan if needed. Never stop a CFTR modulator without talking to your CF provider.

articles.title: Clinical Trials in CF: Frequently Asked Questions
articles.summary: Answers to common questions about participating in cystic fibrosis research.
articles.body:
Clinical trials help researchers test whether new treatments are safe and effective. People with cystic fibrosis of all ages may be eligible for different types of studies, ranging from new medications to surveys and registry projects.

Participation is always voluntary. Before you join a trial, your team will explain the purpose, procedures, risks, and potential benefits. You can ask questions and take time to decide.

Even if you choose not to participate, you still receive the same high-quality care from your CF center. If you’re interested in learning about current studies, ask your team or explore trusted clinical trial registries.

articles.title: Infection Prevention in Everyday Life with CF
articles.summary: Practical steps to lower infection risk at home, at school, and in the community.
articles.body:
Because cystic fibrosis affects the lungs, preventing infections is a major focus of daily life. Handwashing, mask use in certain settings, and avoiding close contact with others who have CF are all part of standard infection prevention guidelines.

At home, clean nebulizers and airway clearance equipment as instructed by your care team. In public settings, consider your comfort level with crowded indoor spaces, especially during cold and flu season.

Talk openly with family, friends, and school or work staff about what you need to feel safe. Clear communication can reduce misunderstandings and help others support your infection prevention plan.

articles.title: Financial Planning for Adults Managing CF Care
articles.summary: Considerations for budgeting, insurance, and disability benefits with CF.
articles.body:
Managing cystic fibrosis often means juggling medical bills, insurance paperwork, and decisions about work and disability benefits. Taking a proactive approach can lower stress and help you plan for the future.

Start by creating a simple budget that includes regular health-related costs such as co-pays, prescriptions, parking, and travel to clinic visits. Track these costs for a few months to see patterns.

Talk with your CF social worker about programs that may help, including copay assistance, charitable foundations, and disability benefits if you’re unable to work full time. You may also want to meet with a financial counselor or planner familiar with chronic illness.

articles.title: How Your Gift Fuels Breakthroughs in CF Research
articles.summary: Learn how donations are transformed into tangible progress for people with CF.
articles.body:
Every donation—large or small—helps move CF research forward. Gifts support laboratory studies, clinical trials, data registries, and quality improvement projects at CF centers across the country.

When you give, you’re helping scientists test new therapies, improve existing treatments, and explore innovative approaches such as gene and cell-based therapies. You’re also supporting the infrastructure that connects researchers, clinicians, and people with CF.

Together, donors have helped extend life expectancy and quality of life for thousands of people living with cystic fibrosis.

articles.title: Advanced Nutrition Strategies for Adults with CF on Modulators
articles.summary: Emerging guidance on adjusting nutrition when starting CFTR modulators.
articles.body:
As more adults with cystic fibrosis start highly effective CFTR modulators, nutrition needs may change. Some people gain weight quickly, while others see more modest changes.

Researchers are studying how modulators affect digestion, blood sugar, and body composition. In the meantime, work closely with your CF dietitian to reassess your calorie needs, vitamin levels, and enzyme dosing. You may eventually be able to adjust your meal plan or supplements based on lab results and how you feel.

care_topics.name: School
care_topics.description: Planning for school accommodations, attendance, and communication with teachers.
default_item_summaries: Review 504 or IEP plan with school staff at least once per year.
Share updated CF care letters with teachers and the school nurse.
Plan for absences due to clinic visits and hospital stays.
Identify a private space and plan for taking medications or doing treatments if needed at school.

care_topics.name: Nutrition
care_topics.description: Daily routines to support healthy weight, growth, and energy.
default_item_summaries: Aim for three meals and two to three snacks each day.
Take pancreatic enzymes with all meals and snacks that contain fat or protein.
Schedule regular visits with a CF dietitian to review growth and lab results.
Keep high-calorie, high-protein snacks available at home and on the go.

care_topics.name: Exercise
care_topics.description: Physical activity plans tailored to lung health and energy levels.
default_item_summaries: Aim for at least 20–30 minutes of physical activity most days, as recommended by your CF team.
Incorporate both aerobic exercise and simple strength training into the weekly routine.
Plan ahead for hydration and extra salt on active days.
Check in with the CF team before starting a new sport or exercise program.

care_topics.name: Mental Health
care_topics.description: Emotional well-being, stress management, and coping skills.
default_item_summaries: Complete recommended depression and anxiety screening at CF clinic visits.
Schedule regular time for an activity that feels relaxing or enjoyable.
Know how to contact a mental health professional or crisis support if needed.
Explore support groups, peer mentoring, or online communities that feel safe and encouraging.

care_topics.name: Medications
care_topics.description: Organizing and taking daily CF medications safely and consistently.
default_item_summaries: Keep an up-to-date medication list and share it at every clinic visit.
Use reminders or pill organizers to support taking medications on schedule.
Order refills early to avoid running out of essential medications.
Store medications safely and according to the instructions on the label.

care_topics.name: Clinic Visits & Monitoring
care_topics.description: Scheduling and preparing for routine CF clinic visits and tests.
default_item_summaries: Attend routine CF clinic visits as recommended by the care team.
Prepare questions or concerns in advance of each appointment.
Track lung function, weight, and other key measures over time.
Schedule recommended vaccines, lab work, and imaging tests.

donation_designations.name: Family Assistance Fund
donation_designations.description: Offers emergency financial assistance for families facing CF-related housing, travel, or utility costs.

donation_designations.name: Clinic Quality Improvement
donation_designations.description: Helps CF care teams implement quality improvement projects that enhance day-to-day care.

donation_designations.name: Wherever the Need Is Greatest
donation_designations.description: Allows the organization to direct your gift to the most urgent needs across programs.

donation_designations.name: Memorial & Tribute Fund
donation_designations.description: Honors loved ones affected by cystic fibrosis through memorial and tribute gifts.

support_group_meetings.group_name: Chicago CF Parents Evening Support Group
support_group_meetings.topic: Balancing CF Care and Family Life
support_group_meetings.description: A monthly in-person support group for parents and caregivers of children with cystic fibrosis in the Chicago area. This session will focus on daily routines, school communication, and making time for your own well-being.
An informal coffee meet-up for parents and caregivers to discuss summer routines, travel with CF, and infection prevention during camp and activities.

support_group_meetings.topic: Advocating for Your Child at School
support_group_meetings.description: Parents and caregivers from Chicagos western suburbs meet to share experiences with 504 plans, IEPs, and working with school teams.
A statewide video support group for parents and caregivers to talk about stress, burnout, and building support networks.

support_group_meetings.group_name: Chicago Adults with CF Peer Group
support_group_meetings.description: In-person discussion group for adults living with CF, focused on work, relationships, and managing treatment routines.

support_group_meetings.group_name: Chicago Teens with CF Connection Group
support_group_meetings.description: A hybrid support group for teens with CF, offering both in-person seating and a secure video option to reduce cross-infection risks.
An informal gathering for people with CF (following infection control guidance), their relatives, and close friends in the Denver area.
A support meeting for parents and caregivers in the Atlanta area focusing on setting realistic goals for the new year.

support_group_meetings.group_name: National Online CF Adult Support Circle
support_group_meetings.description: A national, online-only group for adults living with CF to discuss emotional health, anxiety, and coping skills.
This in-person group focuses on navigating respiratory season, school absences, and infection prevention for children with CF.
A Saturday virtual meeting for caregivers of people with CF to share self-care strategies and community resources.

support_group_meetings.group_name: Boston Adults with CF Hybrid Support Group
support_group_meetings.topic: Working, Studying, and Living with CF
support_group_meetings.description: Hybrid group for adults with CF in the Boston area with limited in-person seating and a virtual option.

volunteer_opportunities.title: CF Walk Packet Stuffing
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Help prepare walker packets for the upcoming Atlanta CF charity walk.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Join staff and fellow volunteers to assemble registration packets, organize T-shirts by size, and prepare signage for the Atlanta CF charity walk. This is a seated, low-lifting role that is great for first-time volunteers.

volunteer_opportunities.title: CF Awareness Booth at Neighborhood Festival
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Staff an information and merchandise table at a local Atlanta spring festival.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Volunteers will help set up and staff a cystic fibrosis awareness booth at a community festival. Tasks include greeting visitors, sharing basic CF facts, and managing a small merchandise table. Some standing is required.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Clinic Welcome Host
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Greet families arriving for CF clinic days at the Atlanta CF center.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: As a clinic welcome host, you will greet families arriving for cystic fibrosis clinic, offer directions, and provide coffee and snacks in the waiting area. Light standing and walking required; training provided on infection control and privacy.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Office Support Volunteer
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Provide weekly administrative support at the Atlanta chapter office.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Help the Atlanta chapter with recurring office tasks such as data entry, preparing mailings, organizing supplies, and answering phone calls. This role is best for volunteers who can commit to a regular weekly schedule.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Route Marking for Chicago CF Walk
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Mark the route and place signage for the Chicago CF charity walk.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Volunteers will meet early on walk day to place mile markers, set up directional signs, and help ensure the route is clearly marked and safe. Some lifting and walking over uneven surfaces are required.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Family Fun Day Activities Helper
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Assist with games and crafts at a CF family fun day in Chicago.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Support staff in running kid-friendly activities at a CF family fun day, including crafts, games, and face painting. Volunteers will help with setup, supervision of activity stations, and cleanup.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Denver CF Gala Setup Crew
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Help set up decorations and auction tables for the CF gala.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Volunteers will assist with unloading supplies, placing centerpieces, arranging auction items, and preparing the venue for the annual CF gala. Moderate lifting may be required.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Advocacy Phone Banking Volunteer
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Call supporters in the Boston area about a CF advocacy campaign.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Work from the chapter office to call supporters, share basic information about current CF advocacy campaigns, and invite them to take action online. Scripts and training will be provided; basic comfort with phones and computers is required.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Merchandise Inventory Helper
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Assist with counting and organizing CF merchandise at the warehouse.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Volunteers will help count, sort, and label boxes of CF awareness merchandise. Tasks include light lifting, scanning barcodes, and updating inventory sheets.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Virtual Social Media Awareness Team
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Help create and share CF awareness content from home.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Join a team of volunteers who help draft posts, design graphics, and share CF awareness messages on social media. Most work is done remotely with monthly virtual check-in meetings.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Saturday Morning Office Filing
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Help file paperwork and organize volunteer records at the Atlanta office.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Support staff with light administrative tasks including filing, shredding outdated documents, and organizing volunteer and event binders. This is a quiet, seated role with flexible breaks.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Virtual Encouragement Card Writing
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Write virtual encouragement cards for teens with CF.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: From home, volunteers will create digital cards and brief messages of encouragement for teens living with CF, which staff will review and deliver electronically. A short online orientation covers privacy and appropriate messaging.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Caregiver Respite Support Volunteer
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Provide short breaks for caregivers of children with CF in Houston.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Trained volunteers offer supervised play and activities at a hospital family resource center so caregivers can attend appointments, run errands, or rest nearby. Background checks and training are required; weekly or twice-monthly shifts preferred.
volunteer_opportunities.venue_name: Houston Medical Center Family Resource Room

events.title: Chicago CF Hope Walk
events.short_description: Join families, friends, and care teams for a 5K walk along the lakefront to raise funds for cystic fibrosis research and support.
events.full_description:
The Chicago CF Hope Walk is a family-friendly 5K along the beautiful lakefront. Funds raised support vital CF research, clinical care, and family support services.

The event includes a 5K walk, kids activity zone, music, and light refreshments. Strollers are welcome. Registered participants receive a walk bib and access to fundraising tools.

Check-in opens one hour before the walk start. Please arrive early to allow time for parking, check-in, and warm-up.

events.title: Chicago Riverfront CF Walk & Family Festival
events.short_description: Scenic 3K riverfront walk followed by a family festival with games, food trucks, and live music.
events.full_description:
Enjoy a relaxed 3K walk along the Chicago Riverwalk to support people living with cystic fibrosis. After the walk, stay for a family festival with games, food trucks, and live music.

This event is stroller-friendly and accessible. T-shirts are guaranteed for participants who register at least three weeks before event day.

events.title: Suburban Chicago CF Trail Walk
events.short_description: A 5K walk on shaded forest preserve trails in the western suburbs to benefit CF programs.
events.full_description:
Gather with other CF supporters for a 5K walk through scenic forest preserve trails. The route includes rest stops, water stations, and volunteer course marshals.

All ages are welcome. Friendly dogs on leashes may join the walk. Please plan to arrive at least 30 minutes before the start time for parking and check-in.

events.title: Aurora Superhero CF Walk
events.short_description: Dress as your favorite superhero and walk to support CF families in the Fox Valley region.
events.full_description:
Put on your cape and join CF families and supporters for a superhero-themed walk at Phillips Park in Aurora. Costumes are encouraged but not required.

The event features a short loop walk suitable for all ages, plus kids activities and a superhero costume parade.

events.title: Milwaukee Lakeside CF Walk
events.short_description: A 5K lakeside walk to support CF care and research in Wisconsin.
events.full_description: Walk along Milwaukees beautiful lakefront in support of people living with CF across Wisconsin. The route is mostly flat and accessible, with rest stops along the way.

events.title: Chicago CF Lakefront VIP Walk
events.short_description: Smaller, premium fundraising walk with a higher registration fee and post-walk brunch.
events.full_description:
This VIP lakefront walk offers a smaller, more intimate fundraising experience, followed by a catered brunch and speaker program. All proceeds support advanced CF research initiatives.

Because of the brunch and venue, registration fees are higher than our standard walks.

events.title: Atlanta CF Strides Walk
events.short_description: Community walk through downtown Atlanta in support of CF research and care.
events.full_description: Join the Atlanta CF community for a 5K walk through downtown and nearby parks. The route features scenic city views and is accessible for strollers and wheelchairs.

events.title: Chicago CF Research & Care Symposium
events.short_description: A half-day education program featuring CF clinicians, researchers, and community voices.
events.full_description:
This free education event highlights the latest in CF research, treatments, and care guidelines. Sessions include talks from CF physicians, dietitians, social workers, and people living with CF.

Advance registration is required; seating is limited. A light breakfast and box lunch will be provided.

events.title: Denver Breathe Easy Gala
events.short_description: An elegant evening gala with dinner, auctions, and stories from the CF community.
events.full_description: The Denver Breathe Easy Gala raises critical funds for CF research and support programs. Guests will enjoy a seated dinner, silent and live auctions, and powerful stories from people living with CF and their families.

events.title: Virtual CF 101: Newly Diagnosed Family Series
events.short_description: A three-part virtual education series for families recently diagnosed with CF.
events.full_description:
This free online series covers CF basics, treatment routines, nutrition, infection prevention, and emotional support resources. Each session includes time for Q&A with CF care team members.

Register once to receive the link and reminders for all three sessions.

events.title: Chicago Great Strides CF Walk 2025
events.short_description: Last years signature Chicago CF walk event.
events.full_description: The 2025 Chicago Great Strides CF Walk brought together hundreds of walkers to support CF research and patient programs. This event has concluded; details remain available for reference.
